ui.aiNote: AI estimate from the knowledge base — not a final conclusion

evidence.notVerified: Not yet verified against the knowledge base
evidence.hint: How many found fragments support the hypothesis, contradict it, or merely mention the topic without a direct conclusion.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page does not exist or has been moved. Check the address or return to the home page.

forbidden.title: Insufficient permissions
forbidden.description: This section is available to a different role. If you need access, contact the administrator.

serverError.title: Something went wrong
serverError.description: An unexpected error occurred. We already know about the problem — try refreshing the page.

glossary.goal: A goal is a production task: what to improve and which indicator measures it (for example, “cut Ni losses in tailings to 8%”). The system selects and checks hypotheses for each goal.
glossary.theme: A topic is a group of similar documents from the knowledge base that the system assembles automatically. Topics are the "raw material" from which hypotheses are born for your goal.
glossary.hypothesis: A hypothesis is a testable idea proposed by the system for your goal: with a rationale, a readiness assessment and links to sources from the knowledge base.
glossary.landscape: The topic map shows every topic of the knowledge base in one picture: where there is more novelty and maturity, which topics are larger. It helps you see the whole picture and pick where to dig.
glossary.trl: Technology readiness (TRL) is a maturity scale from 1 to 9: 1 — just an idea, 9 — a finished, field-proven solution. Assessed per GOST R 58048-2017.
glossary.itc: The technology index is an assessment of a direction's potential from 1 to 10. Computed automatically from the knowledge base: how many works exist on the topic, how new they are and how influential. The higher, the more mature and visible the direction.
glossary.verify: Verification against the knowledge base: the system searches the uploaded documents for passages that support or contradict the hypothesis and issues a verdict. This is an AI assessment based on the available sources, not a final scientific conclusion.
glossary.specialties: Scientific specialties per official classifiers: GRNTI (the scientific-technical information rubricator), VAK (dissertation specialties) and Scopus/ASJC (the international classification). They help understand which field a hypothesis belongs to.
glossary.ranking: Why the hypothesis is at the top: the final rating is computed not by a model but by a transparent formula of six factors (KPI fit, evidence base, novelty, value, risk manageability, readiness). The LLM only extracts features — the weights and the total can be changed.
glossary.cmpp: A cause-and-effect chain: how a controllable factor leads to the target indicator. For materials — composition → process → microstructure → property → KPI. It shows the domain logic, not just text.
glossary.experiment: An experiment plan: what to vary, which characterization and testing methods to verify with, and what the measurable success criterion is. It makes the hypothesis testable in the lab.
glossary.feasibility: A feasibility check: a sober assessment across aspects — science, scaling, safety/ecology, supply chain — with a risk level. It filters out obviously unrealizable ideas.
